---
title: "Frameworks"
subtitle: "Versioned, weighted scoring schemes — weights are data, not code"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.chapter-chips}
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">5 frameworks</span>
<span class="chip chip-grade-c">All draft / illustrative</span>
:::

::: {.section-lead}
Frameworks are the project's **models**: named, versioned schemes that turn defined inputs into a
score. Their weights live in YAML so they can be tuned, audited, and version-controlled independently
of the analysis code. Each framework's logic is implemented once in `src/scoring.py` and guarded by
tests in `tests/`.
:::

> All framework weights at **v0.1 are starting hypotheses**, not validated models. Changing a weight
> should be accompanied by a rationale and, ideally, evidence.

The cards below are generated directly from the framework YAML files — a single source of truth, not a
hand-maintained copy.

```{python}
#| label: framework-cards
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib, html
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))

from src.utils import load_framework
from IPython.display import HTML

# Presentation metadata not stored in the YAML (tests owner). Kept here, close to the view.
META = {
    "market_prioritisation": {
        "name": "Market Prioritisation",
        "output": "Priority score, 0–100 (one per market segment)",
        "tests": "tests/test_scoring.py · tests/test_data_integrity.py",
        "owner": "Commercial Intelligence Lab",
    },
    "customer_fit_score": {
        "name": "Customer Fit Score",
        "output": "Fit score, 0–100 (one per customer)",
        "tests": "tests/test_scoring.py",
        "owner": "Commercial Intelligence Lab",
    },
    "ai_readiness_index": {
        "name": "AI Readiness Index",
        "output": "Readiness score, 0–100 (one per customer)",
        "tests": "tests/test_scoring.py",
        "owner": "Commercial Intelligence Lab",
    },
    "positioning_canvas": {
        "name": "Positioning Canvas",
        "output": "Positioning score, 0–100 (one per candidate framing)",
        "tests": "tests/test_scoring.py (weighted_score)",
        "owner": "Commercial Intelligence Lab",
    },
    "pricing_model": {
        "name": "Pricing Model",
        "output": "Packaging + price tiers (structure only at v0.1)",
        "tests": "— (placeholder; no scoring logic yet)",
        "owner": "Commercial Intelligence Lab",
    },
}
ORDER = ["market_prioritisation", "customer_fit_score", "ai_readiness_index",
         "positioning_canvas", "pricing_model"]

def esc(x):
    return html.escape(str(x))

def status_chip(status):
    cls = {"draft": "chip-grade-c", "placeholder": "chip-placeholder",
           "active": "chip-grade-a"}.get(status, "chip-neutral")
    return f'<span class="chip {cls} no-dot">{esc(status)}</span>'

def field(label, value_html):
    return (f'<div class="fc-field"><div class="fc-label">{esc(label)}</div>'
            f'<div class="fc-val">{value_html}</div></div>')

cards = []
for key in ORDER:
    fw = load_framework(key)
    meta = META[key]

    criteria = fw.get("criteria") or []
    if criteria:
        inputs = ", ".join(f'<code>{esc(c["name"])}</code>' for c in criteria)
        assumptions = ("Criterion weights sum to 1.0; <code>lower_better</code> criteria are "
                       "inverted before weighting. Sub-scores on a 0–10 scale.")
    else:
        dims = fw.get("dimensions") or []
        inputs = ", ".join(f'<code>{esc(d["name"])}</code>' for d in dims) or "—"
        assumptions = "No working model yet; requires willingness-to-pay evidence first."

    notes = fw.get("notes") or fw.get("guardrails") or []
    limitations = "<br>".join(esc(n) for n in notes) if notes else "—"

    body = (field("Purpose", esc(fw.get("question", "—")))
            + field("Inputs", inputs)
            + field("Output", esc(meta["output"]))
            + field("Assumptions", assumptions)
            + field("Tests", f'<code>{esc(meta["tests"])}</code>')
            + field("Limitations", limitations))

    card = (
        '<div class="framework-card">'
        '<div class="fc-head">'
        f'<p class="fc-title">{esc(meta["name"])}</p>'
        f'{status_chip(fw.get("status", "draft"))}'
        f'<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">v{esc(fw.get("version", "0.1.0"))}</span>'
        f'<p class="fc-q">{esc(fw.get("question", ""))}</p>'
        '</div>'
        f'<div class="fc-body">{body}</div>'
        '<div class="fc-meta">'
        f'<span>Owner · {esc(meta["owner"])}</span>'
        f'<span>Stage · {esc(fw.get("lifecycle_stage", "model"))}</span>'
        f'<span>File · <code>frameworks/{esc(key)}.yml</code></span>'
        '</div>'
        '</div>'
    )
    cards.append(card)

HTML('<div class="framework-grid">' + "".join(cards) + "</div>")
```

## How scoring works

The relevant `src/scoring.py` function reads a data row, rescales each sub-score, applies the
`direction` (`higher_better` or `lower_better`), multiplies by the criterion `weight`, sums, and
returns a value in `[0, 100]`. Missing values are handled safely (treated as the neutral midpoint) so
a partial row never crashes the calculation.

## Adding a framework

1. Create `frameworks/<name>.yml` following the shape above (`id`, `version`, `question`, `criteria`
   with `weight` + `direction`, `scale`, `status`).
2. Ensure the weights sum to 1.0 (or document the normalisation rule).
3. If it introduces a new score, add a scoring function to `src/scoring.py` and a test to
   `tests/test_scoring.py` asserting the 0–100 range and missing-value safety.
4. Reference the framework from a dashboard and/or working-paper chapter.

## Related

- [Market Prioritisation dashboard](../dashboards/market-prioritisation.qmd)
- [AI Readiness Scorecard](../dashboards/ai-readiness-scorecard.qmd)
- [Positioning dashboard](../dashboards/positioning-dashboard.qmd)
- [Commercial Intelligence Specification](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md)
